steps.title: Use the domain
steps.content: Both functions have base $1.4>1$, so they increase as $x$ increases. Since $x\ge0$, each function has its minimum at $x=0$.

steps.title: Check function g
steps.content: For $g(x)=18(1.16)(1.4)^{x+2}$, the minimum is $$g(0)=18(1.16)(1.4)^2$$ This value is not displayed as a single constant or coefficient in the equation for $g$.

steps.title: Check function h
steps.content: For $h(x)=18(1.4)^{x+4}$, the minimum is $$h(0)=18(1.4)^4$$ This value is not displayed as a single constant or coefficient in the equation for $h$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Neither equation displays the minimum value as a constant or coefficient, so D is correct.
